span.p: About to baseline on measured data

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Is the metric operationally DEFINED?
same input → same recorded value, any person

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
STOP. Write the operational definition first.
An ambiguous metric makes every later number noise.

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Gage R&R study —
route the %R&R inference to applied-statistics

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Attribute agreement analysis —
do appraisers agree with the standard + each other?

span.p:
Conditionally usable;
improve if it gates a key decision

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Fix the measurement system
BEFORE collecting more data
